hawkish_phrases: Inflation had risen, largely reflecting transitory factors
a substantial majority of participants judged that the risks to their inflation projections were tilted to the upside
Several participants expressed concern that longer-term inflation expectations might rise to inappropriate levels if elevated inflation readings persisted
Various participants mentioned that they expected the conditions for beginning to reduce the pace of asset purchases to be met somewhat earlier than they had anticipated at previous meetings
Several participants saw benefits to reducing the pace of these purchases more quickly or earlier than Treasury purchases in light of valuation pressures in housing markets
dovish_phrases: The Committee's standard of 'substantial further progress' was generally seen as not having yet been met
Many participants remarked, however, that the economy was still far from achieving the Committee's broad-based and inclusive maximum-employment goal
Several participants emphasized, however, that uncertainty around the economic outlook was elevated and that it was too early to draw firm conclusions
participants expected the rise in inflation this year to be transitory
The Committee decided to keep the target range for the federal funds rate at 0 to 1/4 percent
summary: The FOMC unanimously voted to hold the federal funds rate at 0-0.25% and continue asset purchases ($80B Treasuries, $40B MBS monthly), with a mildly hawkish bias as several participants noted conditions for tapering may be met earlier than expected amid inflation concerns, though substantial further progress toward goals has not yet been achieved.
